core_mechanism: thresholding a pairwise correlation (similarity) matrix to build a graph and tracking how connected components (clusters) merge and fragment as the threshold is swept upward — a percolation-style transition on a similarity network
brief_reason: The abstract's central result is that at threshold 0.6 the indices split into clusters and at 0.9 only the most tightly linked ones remain connected, with weaker-correlated units disconnecting first, which is exactly the appearance/breakup of connected components as a correlation threshold is varied on a graph built from a correlation matrix (multifractal MF-DFA/BMFM and RMT eigenvector analysis are secondary components).
